tools.name: server_info

tools.name: sherlock_guide
tools.description: Sherlock/OAK guide and command renderer behind one MCP entrypoint.

tools.name: slurm_guide
tools.description: SLURM workflow guide and command renderer (any SLURM cluster via profile). Profile selected by pi_group (legacy) or BR_SLURM_PROFILE env var. Ships with sherlock_default profile; add your own at configs/slurm/profiles/<name>.yaml (see docs/hpc.md).

tools.name: sherlock_slurm
tools.description: Render, validate, and patch Sherlock sbatch scripts; inspect jobs; read logs; and diagnose job failures.

tools.name: slurm_submit
tools.description: Render / validate / patch sbatch scripts, inspect jobs, read logs, and diagnose failures. Despite the name, this tool does NOT submit jobs - it is read-mostly and helps you author a correct sbatch script. Run sbatch yourself after reviewing the rendered text. Profile selected via cluster_profile parameter or BR_SLURM_PROFILE env var.

tools.description: Search Brain Researcher tools by capability keywords.

tools.description: Get a tool spec; optionally enrich with JSON schema for parameters.

tools.name: get_execution_recipe
tools.description: Return a stateless execution recipe for a public MCP tool or workflow.

tools.description: Create a read-only plan contract with display and execution envelopes.

tools.name: get_latest_plan
tools.description: Return the latest validated Brain Researcher handoff block.

tools.name: tool_execute
tools.description: [DEPRECATED for Agents] Execute a single tool (manual/admin path only).

tools.name: pipeline_plan_validate
tools.description: Validate/normalize a pipeline plan (no execution).

tools.name: pipeline_execute
tools.description: [DEPRECATED for Agents] Execute a validated pipeline plan (manual/admin path only).

tools.name: log_research_event
tools.description:
Persist a lightweight research workflow event for later session/workflow analysis.

    `session_id` should be the canonical Brain Researcher session identifier for one
    continuous coding session. If the client only has a native thread/chat id, pass
    it via `client_session_id`; BR will derive a namespaced canonical `session_id`
    when `session_id` is omitted.

tools.name: write_session_snapshot
tools.description:
Persist a structured end-of-session snapshot for later resume/coaching workflows.

    Use one snapshot per continuous coding session. If the client only exposes a
    native thread/chat id, pass it via `client_session_id`; BR will derive a
    canonical `session_id` when `session_id` is omitted.

tools.name: research_session_digest
tools.description: Return a structured digest for one research-logging session.

tools.name: research_log_summary
tools.description: Summarize persisted research-logging sessions across runs.

tools.name: session_risk_classify
tools.description: Classify handoff risks and hygiene issues for one research session.

tools.name: session_lesson_extract
tools.description: Extract conservative candidate lessons from one research session.

tools.name: session_open_risks_query
tools.description: Query repeated open-risk labels and session hygiene issues.

tools.name: session_policy_cards_generate
tools.description: Generate candidate policy cards from repeated session lessons.

tools.name: session_learning_report_generate
tools.description: Generate a periodic agent-learning report from research sessions.

tools.name: session_signal_report_generate
tools.description: Generate read-only session signals for silent-fail and loop closure mining.

tools.name: session_backfill_to_kg
tools.description: Build or write KG rows for research sessions.

tools.name: request_scientific_review
tools.description: Route scientific review requests to the correct existing review surface.

tools.name: qsm_implementation_review
tools.description: Audit generated QSM code for direct-inversion and local-field dataflow hazards.

tools.name: grounding_resolve
tools.description: Resolve one evidence reference anchor to support/provenance.

tools.name: grounding_gate_evidence_basis
tools.description: Gate evidence_basis rows before final output; weak or unresolved anchors are downgraded.

tools.name: run_get
tools.description: Get run status and step records.

tools.name: run_bundle_get
tools.description: Return a normalized observation bundle for a persisted run.

tools.name: run_scorecard
tools.description: Return a normalized scorecard for a persisted run.

tools.name: run_compare
tools.description: Compare two runs using the normalized MCP scorecard profile.

tools.name: generate_research_trajectory_and_insights
tools.description: Summarize one run/candidate trajectory and persist the result as BR artifacts.

tools.name: generate_bug_digest
tools.description: Summarize the dominant bug/root-cause state for one run/candidate anchor.

tools.name: generate_repo_repair_context
tools.description: Build an agent-readable repo repair context artifact from autoresearch state.

tools.name: run_list
tools.description: List recent runs stored under RUN_ROOT (plus read aliases).

tools.name: run_find_latest_reviewable
tools.description:
Find recent runs that look suitable for scientific review/report generation.

    This helper searches recent persisted runs, skips obvious housekeeping/report
    runs, and returns the newest candidates that expose review-oriented artifacts
    such as ``analysis_bundle.json`` or ``source_summary.json``. The selected
    ``run_id`` can then be passed to ``scientific_report_generate``.

tools.name: run_logs
tools.description: List log files under a run directory.

tools.name: run_request_summary
tools.description: Summarize historical MCP request types from persisted run artifacts.

tools.name: run_cancel
tools.description: Request cancellation of a run (best-effort).

tools.name: run_metrics
tools.description: Return timing/cost metrics for a run and its steps.

tools.name: scientific_report_generate
tools.description:
Draft a scientific report by reviewing one run/workspace and rendering LaTeX.

    When ``run_id`` or ``autoresearch_dir`` is supplied, the tool delegates
    review to the existing scientific-review MCP functions, converts the
    resulting verdict into conservative report sections, merges optional
    plain-text analysis sections, then calls ``latex_report_render``. When no
    source is supplied, it degrades to a plain ``latex_report_render`` call over
    the provided analysis sections and skips scientific review.

    On successful render, the tool also writes a Markdown draft and a
    ``report_revision_handoff`` manifest next to the generated TeX/style/template/PDF artifacts.
    The handoff is a source-preserving contract for a coding agent: retrieve the
    Markdown, TeX, LaTeX style/template assets, PDF, metadata, and handoff manifest; revise the TeX without
    deleting or summarizing BR-generated content; add requested scientific
    details, citations, figures, script paths, and provenance; then compile and
    validate the revised PDF. Optional ``revision_instructions``,
    ``revision_source_artifacts``, ``local_workspace``, and
    ``local_workspace_manifest`` are persisted into that handoff so callers can
    explicitly name details, local files, or prior report sources the coding agent
    must restore. ``local_workspace`` is a coding-agent handoff pointer only; the
    deployed MCP server does not assume it can read that filesystem path.

revision_instructions: Restore figure captions and experiment-log provenance.

required_revision: Read local workspace files before editing TeX.
Add local figure paths, script paths, and provenance.

revision_instructions: Restore figure captions and experiment-log provenance.

analysis_sections.Results Summary: Draft a plain report without scientific review.

tools.name: latex_report_render
tools.description: Render structured content into the bundled Brain Researcher LaTeX report template.

sections_are_latex.description: Interpret section values as raw LaTeX instead of escaping them as plain text.
sections_are_latex.title: Sections Are Latex

sections.Summary: This section may contain normal report prose.

tools.description: List files under the run's artifacts directory.

tools.name: artifact_get_metadata
